seo.title: Building Chain-of-thought and reasoning prompts with Open...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Chain-of-thought and reasoning prompts with OpenAI and Anthropic Claude.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to implementing effective prompt engineering workflows for LLM integration, focusing on reliability, cost efficiency, and maintainability across model updates and providers.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of machine learning model behavior
Familiarity with Python and prompt engineering frameworks

steps.title: Define task constraints and output format
steps.explanation: Explicitly specify input requirements, output structure, and validation criteria before drafting prompts. Use JSON schema for structured outputs and define edge case handling rules.

steps.title: Structure system and user messages
steps.explanation: Separate model instructions (system message) from user input. Use LangChain's PromptTemplate to create reusable components with variable placeholders.

code.content:
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate

system_prompt = PromptTemplate(
    input_variables=["context"],
    template="You are an AI assistant. {context}"
)
user_prompt = PromptTemplate(
    input_variables=["query"],
    template="{query}"
)

pitfalls: Overloading system messages with too many instructions
Missing context variables in template parameters

steps.title: Implement few-shot examples
steps.explanation: Include 2-5 annotated examples in the prompt to establish pattern expectations. Use the 'example' template format for consistency across models.

steps.title: Test with diverse input sets
steps.explanation: Create validation suites with normal, edge, and adversarial cases. Use LangSmith to track prompt performance metrics and error patterns.
pitfalls: Using identical test cases across model versions

steps.title: Optimize for token efficiency
steps.explanation: Trim redundant context and use compact representations. Implement dynamic prompt trimming that maintains core constraints while reducing token count.
pitfalls: Removing critical context for token savings

steps.title: Version prompt artifacts
steps.explanation: Store prompt templates, test suites, and evaluation metrics in version control. Use semantic versioning for prompt iterations with clear changelog entries.

code.content:
# prompt_v1.2.0
# Changes: Added confidence scoring, updated example format
system_prompt: ...

content.conclusion: Implementing structured prompt engineering requires systematic testing, version control, and continuous validation. Prioritize explicit constraints, thorough testing, and maintainable artifact storage to ensure reliable LLM integration.
